section.heading: Commercial product
section.chapeau: In this subtitle, the term “commercial product” means any of the following:

paragraph.chapeau: A product, other than real property, that—

subparagraph.content: is of a type customarily used by the general public or by nongovernmental entities for purposes other than governmental purposes; and
has been sold, leased, or licensed, or offered for sale, lease, or license, to the general public.
evolved from a product described in paragraph (1) through advances in technology or performance; and
is not yet available in the commercial marketplace but will be available in the commercial marketplace in time to satisfy the delivery requirements under a Federal Government solicitation.

paragraph.chapeau: A product that would satisfy the criteria in paragraph (1) or (2) were it not for—

subparagraph.content: modifications of a type customarily available in the commercial marketplace; or
minor modifications made to meet Federal Government requirements.

paragraph.content: Any combination of products meeting the requirements of paragraph (1), (2), or (3) that are of a type customarily combined and sold in combination to the general public.

A product, or combination of products, referred to in paragraphs (1) through (4), even though the product, or combination of products, is transferred between or among separate divisions, subsidiaries, or affiliates of a contractor.

paragraph.chapeau: A nondevelopmental item if the procuring agency determines, in accordance with conditions in the Federal Acquisition Regulation, that—

subparagraph.content: the product was developed exclusively at private expense; and
has been sold in substantial quantities, on a competitive basis, to multiple State and local governments or to multiple foreign governments.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 115–232, div. A, title VIII, § 836(a)(1), Aug. 13, 2018, 132 Stat. 1859.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 103, Pub. L. 111–350, § 3, Jan. 4, 2011, 124 Stat. 3679; Pub. L. 115–91, div. A, title VIII, § 847(a), Dec. 12, 2017, 131 Stat. 1487, defined term “commercial item”, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 115–232, div. A, title VIII, § 836(a)(1), Aug. 13, 2018, 132 Stat. 1859, effective Jan. 1, 2020. See Effective Date note below.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective, and repeal of former section 103 effective, on Jan. 1, 2020, subject to a savings provision, see section 836(h) of Pub. L. 115–232, set out as an Effective Date of 2018 Amendment; Savings Provision note under section 453b of Title 6, Domestic Security.
